01_initial_request.target_name: protein stability changes upon missense mutations
01_initial_request.input_data: FoldX energy terms, molecular dynamics simulation snapshots, and biochemical properties of mutated residues
01_initial_request.user_intent: develop a statistical framework to quantify the uncertainty of predicted changes in protein stability
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: upper bounds on uncertainty of ±2.9 kcal/mol for folding stability and ±3.5 kcal/mol for binding stability

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein stability prediction requires experimental data for model training and validation. [Gap] Need curated datasets with experimentally-determined ΔΔG values for both folding and binding stability. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive model training, chose to use ProTherm and Skempi databases to select protein systems with known experimental stability changes.

parameters.selection_criteria: both stabilizing and destabilizing mutations, more than 20 mutations not all to alanine

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Selected 10 protein systems from ProTherm for folding stability and 10 protein complex systems from Skempi for binding stability with experimentally-determined ΔΔG values

references.citation: Kumar MDS, et al. ProTherm and ProNIT: thermodynamic databases for proteins and protein–nucleic acid interactions. Nucl Acids Res. 2006
references.relevance: Source database for folding stability experimental data

references.citation: Moal IH, Fernández-Recio J. SKEMPI: a Structural Kinetic and energetic database of Mutant Protein Interactions. Bioinformatics. 2012
references.relevance: Source database for binding stability experimental data

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw PDB structures require preparation before computational analysis. [Gap] Structure files need editing to remove unnecessary chains, fix missing residues, and standardize nomenclature. [Decision] To achieve proper structure preparation, chose to use PDB file editing workflow to prepare structures for MD simulation and FoldX analysis.

parameters.operations: editing out unnecessary chains, fixing missing residues, standardizing nomenclature
parameters.source: PDB website using PDB IDs from Table 1

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Prepared structure files ready for MD simulation and FoldX analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Single static structures do not represent conformational variability under physiological conditions. [Gap] Need to sample configurational variation of proteins to improve prediction accuracy. [Decision] To achieve conformational sampling, chose to use GROMACS 5.0.3 to perform 100 ns-long MD simulations on prepared structures.

parameters.purpose: sample configurational variation of proteins observed under physiological conditions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated MD simulation trajectories for all 20 protein systems

references.citation: Miller CR, et al. Initiating a watch list for Ebola virus antibody escape mutations. PeerJ. 2016
references.relevance: Previous work describing MD simulation protocol details

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MD trajectories contain continuous conformational data that needs to be discretized for analysis. [Gap] Need representative structures from MD simulation for FoldX calculations. [Decision] To achieve structural sampling, chose to capture 100 snapshots from each MD simulation at 1 ns intervals.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Captured 100 snapshots from each MD simulation for subsequent FoldX analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FoldX calculates ΔΔG as linear combination of energy terms fitted to experimental data. [Gap] Need to calculate stability changes for all mutations across all MD snapshots. [Decision] To achieve stability prediction, chose to use FoldX 4.0 to analyze each snapshot and calculate ΔΔG of folding and binding.

parameters.output: ΔΔG values per mutation from each snapshot
parameters.energy_terms: van der Waals energy, solvation energy, entropy terms, etc.
parameters.averaging: ΔΔG values averaged across 100 snapshots to obtain final ΔΔG

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated FoldX-predicted ΔΔG values with energy term breakdowns and standard deviations from MD snapshot averaging

references.citation: Schymkowitz J, et al. The FoldX web server: an online force field. Nucleic Acids Res. 2005
references.relevance: FoldX program description and energy function basis

references.citation: Guerois R, et al. Predicting changes in the stability of proteins and protein complexes. J Mol Biol. 2002

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Need to compare MD-based predictions with single-structure predictions to assess improvement. [Gap] Baseline comparison requires FoldX analysis on experimental structures without MD sampling. [Decision] To achieve comparison baseline, chose to use FoldX 4.0 on single experimental structure files as control/ablation.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated single-structure FoldX ΔΔG predictions for comparison with MD-based predictions

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Need to build statistical models to predict uncertainty of FoldX predictions. [Gap] Must determine optimal model selection method between stepwise and best subset approaches. [Decision] To achieve model selection comparison, chose to use R 4.1 stepwise selection method as initial approach.

parameters.method: predictor variables alternatively added and removed one at a time

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated models using stepwise selection, but best subset selection performed better in cross validation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Stepwise selection showed inferior performance compared to alternative methods. [Gap] Need better model selection approach for optimal predictor combination. [Decision] To achieve optimal model selection, chose to use R 4.1 best subset selection method comparing all possible predictor combinations.

parameters.method: compares models with every possible combination of predictor variables

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated optimal models with best BIC scores; best subset selection outperformed stepwise selection

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Models need validation to assess generalizability across protein systems. [Gap] Standard cross-validation may not account for system-specific variation. [Decision] To achieve robust validation, chose to use modified leave-one-system-out cross validation excluding all datapoints from one system as testing set.

parameters.metric: coverage rate and median prediction interval width

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Validated model performance with approximately 95% coverage rate; identified system-specific variation in accuracy

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Final models need to quantify uncertainty bounds for FoldX predictions. [Gap] Need to establish upper bounds on prediction uncertainty for practical use. [Decision] To achieve uncertainty quantification, chose to use linear regression framework with MD snapshots, biochemical properties, and FoldX energy terms to predict error bounds.

parameters.response_variable: Error (absolute difference between ΔΔG_FoldX and ΔΔG_exp)
parameters.predictors_folding: van der Waals energy, van der Waals clash, side chain entropy, SD of total energy, proline involvement, secondary structure
parameters.predictors_binding: van der Waals clash, SD of backbone clash, SD of side chain entropy, SD of total energy, secondary structure, RSA

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Established upper bounds on uncertainty of ±2.9 kcal/mol for folding stability and ±3.5 kcal/mol for binding stability with 95% prediction interval coverage

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Leave-one-system-out cross validation with 95% prediction interval coverage assessment

folding_stability_uncertainty.interpretation: Upper bound on uncertainty for folding stability predictions with 95% coverage

binding_stability_uncertainty.interpretation: Upper bound on uncertainty for binding stability predictions with 95% coverage

coverage_rate.interpretation: Proportion of prediction intervals that capture the experimental Error when tested on unseen data

folding_dataset_size.interpretation: Total number of folding stability mutations in dataset

binding_dataset_size.interpretation: Total number of binding stability mutations in dataset

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: Successfully developed a statistical framework using linear regression to quantify uncertainty of FoldX-predicted protein stability changes, meeting the quantifiable goals of ±2.9 kcal/mol for folding stability and ±3.5 kcal/mol for binding stability upper bounds with 95% prediction interval coverage. MD simulation integration significantly improved model precision compared to single-structure approaches.
